concept.title: Kyma Gateway
concept.shortdesc: Kyma Gateway is a preconfigured Istio Gateway custom resource that describes the ports and protocols exposed for your cluster domain.

section.p: Kyma Gateway is a preconfigured Istio Gateway CR named kyma-gateway located in the kyma-system namespace. It describes the ports and protocols exposed for a particular domain.

note.p: Kyma Gateway is not recommended for production environments. For production use, set up a custom gateway with your own domain and TLS certificate. If you are still deciding which gateway configuration to use, see .

section.title: Overview
section.p: The Kyma Gateway configuration differs between environments as shown in the following table:

row.entry: Managed by a Gardener Certificate CR
Managed by a Gardener DNSEntry CR

section.title: Shared Configuration
section.p: The following settings apply to Kyma Gateway in both SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime and open-source Kyma clusters:

ul.li: Listens on port 443 (HTTPS) using TLS mode SIMPLE with TLS credentials from the kyma-gateway-certs Secret in the istio-system namespace
Listens on port 80 (HTTP) and automatically redirects all requests to HTTPS with a 301 status code
Serves all hosts matching the wildcard pattern *.{domain}, where {domain} is the cluster domain resolved at reconciliation time
Gateway selector targets the default Istio ingress gateway (app: istio-ingressgateway, istio: ingressgateway)
A VirtualService named istio-healthz exposes the Istio readiness endpoint at healthz.{domain}/healthz/ready

subsection.p: In a managed SAP BTP, Kyma runtime cluster, Kyma Gateway uses the Gardener Shoot domain. The TLS certificate and DNS record are managed automatically by the operator using Gardener resources. An Istio VirtualService exposes the Istio readiness endpoint at healthz.{GARDENER_SHOOT_DOMAIN}/healthz/ready.

The cluster domain is resolved from the Gardener shoot-info ConfigMap. The operator creates and manages a DNSEntry CR named kyma-gateway in the kyma-system namespace. The DNSEntry points to the external IP addresses or hostnames of the istio-ingressgateway LoadBalancer Service in the istio-system namespace.
The operator detects the IP stack of the istio-ingressgateway Service by inspecting the spec.ipFamilies field:

The operator creates and manages a Gardener Certificate CR named kyma-tls-cert in the istio-system namespace. The certificate covers all subdomains of the cluster domain (*.{domain}) and automatically stores the TLS data in the kyma-gateway-certs Secret.

In an open-source Kyma cluster, Kyma Gateway uses the local.kyma.dev domain and a pre-populated self-signed certificate. It is intended for local development only. An Istio VirtualService exposes the Istio readiness endpoint at healthz.local.kyma.dev/healthz/ready.
No DNSEntry is created. DNS resolution must be configured externally.

The operator creates a pre-populated Kubernetes Secret named kyma-gateway-certs in the istio-system namespace. This Secret contains a self-signed certificate valid until July 2030 and is intended for local development use with the local.kyma.dev domain.
By default, Kyma Gateway is enabled. To disable it, modify the by removing the enableKymaGateway field or setting it to false:
subsection.codeblock: apiVersion: operator.kyma-project.io/v1alpha1 kind: APIGateway metadata: name: default spec: enableKymaGateway: false
subsection.p: When you disable Kyma Gateway or delete the APIGateway CR, the operator removes all managed resources: Gateway, DNSEntry, Certificate (or certificate Secret), and VirtualService.

You can only disable Kyma Gateway if no APIRule or VirtualService resources in the cluster reference kyma-system/kyma-gateway. If such resources exist, the operator returns a warning listing up to five of them. Remove or migrate those resources to a different gateway before disabling Kyma Gateway.
